summary: Tested IRS 1095-C 1H employee filtering and PDF generation. All 7 backend tests passed (100%). Frontend tests passed - login, IRS Forms page, 1095-C tab shows only offered employees (no 1H), PDF download includes employee name, 1094-C tab works correctly.
passed_tests: Backend: All 1095-C forms have valid offer codes (1B, 1C, 1D, 1E - no 1H)
Backend: GET /api/irs-forms/1095c/{employer_id}/2026/{employee_id}/pdf downloads successfully for offered employees
Backend: GET /api/irs-forms/1094c/{employer_id}/2026 generates correctly with Part I, II, III
Backend: GET /api/irs-forms/summary/{employer_id}/2026 returns accurate data (60 total, 47 FT, 57.25 FTE, ALE)
Backend: Offer code distribution correct - 47 offered (1B/1C/1D/1E), 13 excluded (1H)
Frontend: Login with fajju2001@gmail.com / test123 successful
Frontend: IRS Forms page loads with summary cards showing 47 FT employees, 57.25 FTE, 47 1095-C forms
Frontend: Card correctly shows 'employees offered coverage' label (not 'forms required')
Frontend: Forms 1095-C tab shows 47 forms with valid offer codes (1B, 1C, 1D, 1E - no 1H)
Frontend: Expanded 1095-C row shows Part II monthly grid (All 12 Months + Jan-Dec) with Lines 14, 15, 16
Frontend: Part III shows covered individuals section
Frontend: PDF download works with filename including employee name (1095-C_Alice_Johnson_2026.pdf)
Frontend: Form 1094-C tab shows Part I (ALE Member Info), Part II (ALE Details), Part III (Monthly Data)
critical_code_review_comments: 1095-C batch endpoint correctly filters out 1H employees (line 2344 in server.py)
Single employee and PDF endpoints return 400 for 1H employees with clear error message
render_1095c_pdf uses Canvas-based drawing for official IRS form layout
PDF includes Part I (Employee/Employer info), Part II (monthly grid), Part III (covered individuals)
Frontend correctly shows 'employees offered coverage' label in summary card
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Used existing employer with 60 employees (47 FT offered coverage, 13 PT with 1H excluded)
context_for_next_testing_agent: IRS 1095-C 1H filtering fully tested and working. Key findings: (1) 1095-C batch endpoint filters out employees with offer_code='1H'. (2) Single employee and PDF endpoints return 400 for 1H employees. (3) PDF uses Canvas-based rendering matching official IRS form layout. (4) Frontend shows 47 forms (not 60) because 13 PT employees with 1H are excluded. (5) PDF filename includes employee name (e.g., 1095-C_Alice_Johnson_2026.pdf). (6) Summary card correctly shows 'employees offered coverage' label.

verified_fixes.1h_filtering: 1095-C batch endpoint filters out 1H employees (line 2344 in server.py)
verified_fixes.400_for_1h: Single employee and PDF endpoints return 400 for 1H employees
verified_fixes.pdf_layout: render_1095c_pdf uses Canvas-based drawing for official IRS form layout
verified_fixes.card_label: Summary card shows 'employees offered coverage' instead of 'forms required'
